regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean: Conditions remain stable but breezy on March 8, with easterly winds 8-16 knots and overcast skies common across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos. Rain chances hover around 45-55% today, decreasing after, so expect intermittent showers and reduced visibility, especially near San Juan and St. Thomas harbors. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor local updates as seas remain moderate. Fishing captains should plan for variable light winds and occasional rain; yacht operators should maintain caution when anchoring in exposed bays due to easterly gusts near 15 knots.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean conditions remain favorable today with temperatures near 79 degrees Fahrenheit in Cozumel, easterly winds at 9 knots, and partly cloudy skies. Similar light easterly trades at 9 to 11 knots will prevail through March 10, supporting smooth seas and good visibility across ports including Costa Maya, Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth. Rain chances are minimal, under 5 percent, so expect dry decks and stable anchoring conditions. Captains should maintain standard watch protocols; small craft operators and fishing vessels can safely operate without heightened weather concerns. Passengers are advised to enjoy outdoor activities with sun protection and remain aware of typical marine breeze patterns.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather today features light easterly winds 5 knots and clear skies with temperatures near 71°F across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos. Conditions remain calm with minimal rain chance, ideal for fishing and yacht operations; however, expect a shift to overcast skies and increased easterly winds 9 to 14 knots over the next three days. Mariners should monitor the gradual rise in wind speed, especially near Freeport and Bimini anchorages, where east winds up to 14 knots could cause moderate chop. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities for today while anticipating cooler, drizzly conditions by day three.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean weather for March 08, 2026, remains stable with east winds steady at 20-21 knots and overcast skies across Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena. Expect light seas and minimal rain through day two, with a slight increase in rain showers near Aruba on day three (up to 16% chance). Mariners should note Small Craft Advisory conditions for exposed waters due to sustained east winds near 20 knots; fishing captains are advised to secure light gear and avoid extended offshore operations. Cruise passengers can anticipate mild temperatures near 80-82°F with overcast skies—outdoor activities remain feasible but carry rain gear for possible light showers later in the period. Anchorage in Cartagena and the ABC islands remains safe with moderate wind-driven swell; maintain watch for any localized squalls.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions for March 8, 2026 remain stable with light east-northeast winds around 11 knots and temperatures near 79-81°F across Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Costa Maya. Expect mostly partly cloudy to overcast skies with low rain chances today (15-25%), increasing to isolated showers tomorrow but diminishing by day three. Mariners should maintain normal caution in exposed anchorages such as Grand Cayman’s North Sound, where moderate easterly winds may cause slight chop; no Small Craft Advisories are in effect. Cruise and fishing operators should plan for mild seas and brief interruptions from light showers, with no severe weather threats or tropical disturbances present.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for March 8, 2026: Winds remain steady from the east-northeast at 17-20 knots with partly cloudy to overcast skies. Barbados and nearby ports including St. Lucia, Martinique, and Dominica can expect mild seas and low rain chances today, increasing to isolated showers by midweek. Yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear and maintain vigilant watch as winds approach Small Craft Advisory thresholds by day two, especially near exposed anchorages like St. Vincent and Grenada. Passengers should plan outdoor activities early and remain prepared for brief showers in Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region is currently under clear skies with light north winds at 7 knots and a comfortable 62°F. Expect a strong warming trend today with east winds increasing to 14 knots and highs reaching 89°F, providing excellent conditions for cruises and yacht operations out of Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, and Marina del Rey. Catalina Island will see similar clear, warm weather—ideal for day trips or fishing. Conditions become cooler and overcast on days two and three, with southwest to south winds 8-14 knots and minimal rain chances; mariners should remain alert for reduced visibility and adjust anchorage plans accordingly. No marine wind warnings are in effect.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions today start cool and calm with clear skies, northwest winds at 5 knots, and no precipitation expected. Expect increasing westerly winds tomorrow up to 17 knots with light drizzle near Ensenada and Punta Banda, though rain chances remain below 20 percent. Rain chances rise to around 30 percent on day two with northwest winds near 9 knots; vessels should monitor weather updates for possible localized showers near Islas Todos Santos. Fishing captains should plan for moderate seas and variable cloud cover midweek, while passenger activities ashore can proceed with minimal weather impact today.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports will see steady northerly winds 8-19 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain risk through March 8-10. Mariners should note increasing breeze speeds near La Paz and San Jose del Cabo by day three, but no Small Craft Advisories are expected. Fishing captains can expect calm seas and stable conditions today with good visibility despite cloud cover. Cruise passengers should prepare for mild temperatures around 71-75°F and light winds; outdoor activities remain suitable with no significant weather disruptions forecasted.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions for March 8, 2026: Current temperature 76°F with light SSW winds at 3 knots and overcast skies. Winds will shift west to northwest over the next three days, increasing to 15 knots by day three, accompanied by mostly overcast skies and isolated rain showers, particularly near Topolobampo and Altata. No marine wind warnings are in effect; seas remain moderate with no significant risk for small craft advisories. Captains should monitor wind shifts when approaching anchorage in Topolobampo, as northwest winds may cause some unsettled conditions; fishing operations can proceed with caution under overcast skies and light rain chances. Passengers advised to plan outdoor activities with light rain possible but no severe weather expected.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region remains under stable marine conditions today with temperatures near 78°F and light NNE winds at 3 knots. Expect overcast skies with minimal rain chances through the next three days; winds will shift to northwest 7-9 knots, maintaining calm seas across Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Mariners should find secure anchorage with no significant swell or wind concerns, but fishing captains should monitor cloud cover for reduced visibility during early morning hours. Passengers are advised that overcast skies may limit sun exposure; light jackets are recommended for coastal excursions.
regions.western-mediterranean:
Western Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 08, 2026:

Current conditions in Barcelona show 50°F with light NNW winds at 3 knots and overcast skies; rain chances remain minimal at 5 percent. Expect increasing instability today with ENE winds at 6 knots and a 55 percent chance of rain showers, potentially impacting visibility and deck operations. By March 9, thunderstorms are possible near Valencia and Palma de Mallorca with WSW winds at 5 knots and a 23 percent rain chance; mariners should monitor for sudden gusts and lightning hazards. On March 10, fog is likely near coastal ports including Malaga and Cadiz, requiring reduced speeds and enhanced navigation watch. Yacht and fishing captains should secure loose gear ahead of showers and thunderstorms, while cruise operators should advise passengers to plan outdoor activities accordingly. No marine wind warnings are in effect at this time.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean weather for March 8, 2026, remains stable with light north to northeast winds and overcast skies across key ports from Marseille to Naples and the islands of Sardinia and Corsica. Naples reports mild temperatures near 57°F with minimal rain risk today, increasing slightly to 23% chance tomorrow under similar overcast conditions. Mariners should expect light winds generally under 10 knots, posing no Small Craft Advisory threat. Yacht and fishing captains should maintain standard anchorage protocols in harbors like Genoa, La Spezia, and Dubrovnik; fog may reduce visibility near Venice by day three, so exercise caution when navigating shallow or congested waters. Passengers should plan for cool, damp conditions and limited sun exposure during shore excursions.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 8, 2026:

Athens (Piraeus) reports 54°F with light WNW winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies. Visibility may be reduced by patchy fog tomorrow morning as light NNE winds increase to 5 knots, but no precipitation is expected across the region through the next three days. Ports including Santorini, Mykonos, Rhodes, Corfu, Istanbul, Izmir, Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria should expect mild northerly winds under 7 knots and generally stable conditions with low rain chances. Mariners should anticipate calm seas and good anchorage conditions; fishing captains should plan for light winds and moderate temperatures, while cruise operators can advise passengers to carry light layers for cooler mornings and enjoy mostly clear skies. No marine warnings are in effect at this time.
